### Post by blippy on 2007-11-26
I decided to reinstall Gutsy after sound seemed to get totally screwed. I am slowly working my way towards being able to rebuild my box automatically.

One thing I notices that that /etc/resolv.conf says
# generated by NetworkManager, do not edit!

Is there a way that I can programmatically set the network settings of domain name and DNS servers?

I like ubuntu a lot, but I think that one of its problems is that it hides stuff behind ever-deeper layers of abstractions, and its difficult to fathom out how it's arriving at the stuff it's arriving out.

### Post by scorp123 on 2007-11-26
[http://www.noah.org/wiki/index.php/Resolv.conf](http://www.noah.org/wiki/index.php/Resolv.conf)

---

### Post by blippy on 2007-11-26
Thanks. I'm getting closer. Not quite there yet,

What I did was (approximately)
apt-get install resolvconf sysvconfig

Edit /etc/resolvconf/resolv.conf.d/base to contain the lines:
nameserver 192.168.XXX.YYY
nameserver ...
nameserver ...

Add the following lines to  /etc/networking/interfaces to give me a static IP address:
```
auto eth0
iface eth0 inet static
        address ...
        netmask 255.255.255.0
        network ...
        broadcast ...
        gateway ...
```




Then restart everything:
service networking restart
service resolvconf restart

If I look at /etc/resolv.conf, I'll see the nameservers I set up. If I start Gnome's network manager, then I'll see that I have a static IP address, and the correct nameservers.

There's just one thing missing: how do I set the domain name?

---
